meaning: n.民族志学,人种学,人种志
examples: Ethnography is a typical qualitative research method.
Perhaps you saw “ethnography” and assumed it would just be quaint reports from the Amazon and the South Seas.
Ethnography is a term borrowed from anthropology, meaning the systematic and immersion study of human cultures.
Wesch teaches anthropology to undergrads and heads up a working group on digital ethnography.
That helps explain why, as Wesch notes, digital ethnography is increasingly being integrated into other majors at universities.
